tags.name: Air Wall
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: Wall on southwest/east side of Gili Air, 5-24m (manta-dive-giliair.com) or 8-28m (bluemarlindive.com). Gorgonian sea fans and pygmy seahorses confirmed at this site (manta-dive-giliair.com). Bumphead parrotfish: manta-dive-giliair.com page does NOT list them at Air Wall; only confirmed at Halik. Removed from Air Wall description as unconfirmed. Difficulty should be beginner-to-advanced per local operators but intermediate is reasonable classification. Location described as southwest by manta-dive-giliair.com, northwest by terumbudivers.com, eastern by starfish.ch - sources conflict on exact side of Gili Air. Depth: Manta Dive Air says 5-24m, Divezone and Starfish say max 32m.

tags.name: Bounty
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14 RE-AUDIT: Vessel was a floating pier/pontoon, 30m x 15m. Sinking year CONFLICT: divecentralgili.com says 2001 storm; terumbudivers.com says 'early 2000s'; manta-dive-giliair.com says sunk 1999; divers-guide.com says 2001. Year 2001 is most commonly cited but not fully consistent across sources. Depth 8-18m confirmed by multiple sources. Location: west or southwest of Gili Meno (not north - conflict with existing data). Pygmy seahorses: mentioned by gilidivers.com overview but NOT confirmed by direct wreck-specific pages (manta-dive-giliair.com, divers-guide.com, divecentralgili.com do not list pygmy seahorses at this site). Frogfish, scorpionfish confirmed. Bounty date conflict: divecentralgili.com says 2001 storm, divessi.com says various dates (1999-2003), terumbudivers.com says early 2000s. Three sources agree storm sinking not intentional.

tags.name: Deep Turbo
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: Sea mound/pinnacle north of Gili Trawangan. Depth 18-35m (manta-dive-giliair.com says avg 35m). Bumphead parrotfish confirmed at this site by manta-dive-giliair.com. Pygmy seahorses in sea fans confirmed. Eagle rays, reef sharks, turtles confirmed. 'Advanced and above' classification confirmed. Leopard shark mentioned by bluemarlindive.com. Not eagle ray/pelagic-focused as originally written - sea mound/seamount type is correct.

tags.name: Frog Fish Point
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: Coordinate lon 116.0886 places site east of Gili Air, consistent with starfish.ch which lists 'Gili Air Harbour/Frogfish Point' as a sandy area east of Gili Air. divessi.com lists Frogfish Point near Gili Air. Site features ghost pipefish, frogfish, scorpionfish, shrimps and nudibranchs on coral block 5-18m. Three species of frogfish (warty/giant/hairy) not independently confirmed as distinct from the general critter description - only generic 'frogfish' mentioned. Site type reef is appropriate. Location coordinate plausible.

tags.name: Halik
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: North/northwest Gili Trawangan, 1 km long, 6-40m depth range. Halik and Trawangan Wall appear to describe overlapping areas on the northwest coast of Gili Trawangan. Eagle rays, reef sharks, trevally, barrel sponges, anemone fields confirmed. Bumphead parrotfish: divecentralgili.com states 'during a full moon, bumphead parrotfish school in huge numbers can be seen' - confirmed but seasonal/conditional. Deep Halik extends to 40m (advanced section). Drift dive type confirmed.

tags.name: Jack Point
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: DIFFICULTY CORRECTED from Intermediate to Advanced. Site is on the NORTH side of Gili Trawangan (not west as original description stated). Depth 24-35m+. Classified as 'Advanced divers only' by manta-dive-giliair.com. Two coral ridges extending west, separated by white sand at 24m. Big-eye trevally, whitetip and blacktip reef sharks, snappers, occasional eagle rays and manta rays. Best site for pelagic encounters in the Gilis. Also suitable for Deep Specialty and Nitrox. No jackfish at a west headland - actual site is two deep ridges heading north.

tags.name: Malang
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: Takat Malang = 'bent reef' in Indonesian per manta-dive-giliair.com. Located just north of Gili Air (not north of all Gili Islands as previously stated). Reef top at 10m, drops to 35m (manta-dive-giliair.com) or 40m (multiple other sources). Long winding reef with undulating valleys. 10 major takats per some sources; manta-dive-giliair.com describes it differently as a ridge system. Pygmy seahorses in gorgonian fans: claimed in original description but NOT confirmed by manta-dive-giliair.com's actual site page - removed. Garden eels and blue-spotted stingrays on sandy bottom confirmed. Schooling fish confirmed.

tags.name: Manta Point
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: South side of Gili Trawangan, same site as 'Sunset Reef' per manta-dive-giliair.com URL structure. Depth 5-24m. Manta rays seasonal (rainy season/April-November per terumbudivers.com; December-March per bluemarlindive.com for Sunset Reef - conflicting seasons). Rarely strong current. Reef sharks, turtles, cuttlefish, octopus confirmed. Site type: fringing reef not drift - but moderate drift current possible. The coordinate (lat -8.363335) is south of Gili Trawangan consistent with this placement.

tags.name: Meno Corner
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: SITE TYPE CORRECTED from drift to reef (slope + pinnacle). Northeast tip of Gili Meno. Known as 'Turtle Heaven' or 'The Pinnacle' by some operators. Slope leads to pinnacle 9-30m. Turtles (green and hawksbill) in large numbers confirmed. Also called 'Turtle Heaven' by Gili Divers. Suitable for entry-level divers in shallow sections. Moray eels, fusiliers confirmed. The JSON coordinate (lon 116.0625884) is east of Gili Meno center, consistent with northeast placement. 'Drift' classification was incorrect - site is slope/pinnacle reef.

tags.name: Meno Slope
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: West/south of Gili Meno, gentle slope. Oceans5 lists max 20m; osm_clean has 30m. Hawksbill and green turtles confirmed on coral bommies. All levels site. Blue-striped snappers, reef sharks confirmed. Suitable for beginner-through-intermediate divers. Exists as distinct site from Meno Wall.

tags.name: Meno Wall
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: West side of Gili Meno (not northwest/channel between T and Meno as previously described). Wall from 5m to 22-25m sandy bottom. Turtles (hawksbill, green, leatherhead) in large numbers confirmed. Moray eels, lionfish, scorpionfish, grouper confirmed. Bumphead parrotfish: one source (manta-dive.com) says 'look into the blue to spot bumphead parrotfish' - possible but unconfirmed as regular sighting. Bamboo sharks: NOT confirmed by any source - REMOVED. Night diving: confirmed excellent (lobsters, crabs, shrimps per manta-dive search result, Spanish dancer per oceans5). Pygmy seahorses: NOT confirmed at this site - REMOVED. Current: varies greatly per manta-dive search result.

tags.name: Mirkos Reef
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: Between Gili Air and Gili Meno. Depth 12-35m. Deep ridges like fingers extending 35m-18m. Flat reef top at 12m. Giant frogfish among sponges confirmed (manta-dive-giliair.com). Whitetip reef sharks cruising ridges confirmed. Green sea turtles on reef top confirmed. Blue-spotted ribbon-tailed rays confirmed. Schooling fusiliers and snappers confirmed. Also called 'Secret Reef' by some operators. M statue placed in memory of diver named Mirkos. Difficulty rated Intermediate to Advanced by manta-dive-giliair.com.

tags.name: Shallow Turbo
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: This site appears on trawanganstingraydivers.com dive site listing for Gili Trawangan as a beginner site. BioRock structures feature confirmed. All-levels site with channels and BioRock structures. Marine life: turtles, schooling fish, unicorn fish, scorpionfish, trumpet fish, ornate ghost pipefish.

tags.name: Shark Point
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: Northwest or west of Gili Trawangan. Slope from 6m to 30m+ with ridges, canyons, and valleys. Glenn Nusa tugboat wreck confirmed: sunk February 2016 (intentionally, not accidentally). Dimensions conflict: divecentralgili.com says 29m long x 4m wide; manta-dive search result says 22m long x 6m high. Max depth 28m. Ship's chimney at 21m. Features big openings for penetration. Marine life: batfish, snapper schools, whitetip reef sharks, blue-spotted stingrays, pipefish, leaf scorpionfish, nudibranchs confirmed (divecentralgili.com). Green turtles in large numbers at shallow sections. The site suits all levels per manta-dive-giliair.com.

tags.name: Simons Reef
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: North of Gili Meno (not north of all Gili Islands). Depth 25-40m sandy bottom. 8 major takats in a figure-eight shape per bluemarlindive.com. Pygmy seahorses in gorgonian fans: gilidivers.com mentions this as a feature of the site. Schooling fish, barrel sponges confirmed. Also appears at max 40m per divezone.net. Advanced only confirmed. Sea snakes also mentioned by gilidivers.com.

tags.name: Segaluh
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: No site-specific sources found for 'Segaluh'. Coordinate (lon 116.0982787) places this notably east of the Gili Islands, closer to Lombok's northwest coast. No dive operators list this site by name. Origin is OSM Diveschool Notice Board only. Cannot validate or confirm.

tags.name: Teluk NBoro
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: No site-specific sources found under 'Teluk NBoro'. The coordinate (lat -8.4042) is south of the Gili Islands on the Lombok northwest coast, consistent with Teluk Nara/Naro bay area. Teluk Nara is documented as a muck dive site near the Gili Islands ferry jetty (seahorses, ghost pipefish, nudibranchs, octopus, cuttlefish). The name 'NBoro' may be a spelling variant of 'Naro' or 'Nara'. However, without 3 sources specifically naming this site, cannot validate. The site_type should possibly be muck/reef hybrid based on Teluk Nara descriptions.

tags.name: Trawangan Wall
tags.note: source is Diverschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: 'Trawangan Wall' as a named site does not appear independently in local operator dive site lists. Northwest Gili Trawangan reef is consistently called 'Halik' by all major operators (divecentralgili.com, bluemarlindive.com, manta-dive.com, gilidivers.com). Coordinate (lon 116.0436) is on the west/northwest face of Gili Trawangan, same area as Halik. These may be two names for the same or immediately adjacent section of reef. Cannot find 3 independent sources for 'Trawangan Wall' as a distinct named site.

tags.name: Lombok Dive
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: 'Lombok Dive' as a named dive site does not appear in any operator dive site listing found. The coordinate (lat -8.3476, lon 116.0284) places this west of Gili Trawangan. This area corresponds to waters west of the island, near Shark Point or Manta Point approaches. No independent sources found specifically naming this as a distinct dive site.

tags.name: Vila Almarick Biorock
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: Biorock structures on east coast of Gili Trawangan are confirmed as a collective by multiple sources (gili-eco-trust via Wikipedia, bluemarlindive.com 'BioRocks', divecentralgili.com). The Gili Eco Trust Biorock project started in 2004, reached 110+ structures by 2016, now 180+. The Biorock program is well-documented and the individual named structures are part of this established program. The 'world's second-largest Biorock project' claim in the markdown is UNVERIFIED - Wikipedia does not make this claim; Blue Marlin says 'biggest in the world'. Claim removed.

tags.name: Bale Sampan Biorock
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: Part of the east coast Gili Trawangan Biorock cluster. Biorock project confirmed (Wikipedia, bluemarlindive.com, gili-eco-trust). The Biorock program is well-documented and the individual named structures are part of this established program. The claim '120+ frames since 2006' in the markdown is inaccurate - project started 2004 and grew from 63 structures (2010) to 110+ (2016) to 180+ (now). Removed '120 frames since 2006' claim. 'World's second-largest' claim UNVERIFIED - removed.

tags.name: Samba Vilas Biorock
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: Part of the east coast Gili Trawangan Biorock cluster. Biorock project itself is well-documented (started 2004 per Wikipedia, gili-eco-trust) and individual named structures are part of this established program. 'World's second-largest' claim in markdown is UNVERIFIED and removed.

tags.name: Trawangan Dive Biorock
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: Trawangan Dive operator is confirmed real (trawangandive.com) and their Biorock involvement is consistent with known Gili Eco Trust activities. Part of the well-documented Biorock program on the east coast of Gili Trawangan. Named structure is part of the reef restoration program run by multiple dive operators.

tags.name: Gili Divers Biorock
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: Gili Divers operator confirmed (gilidivers.com). Their dive site listing mentions 'Bio Rocks' as a collective site on the east coast of Gili Trawangan. Part of the well-documented Gili Eco Trust Biorock program. Named structure is part of this established reef restoration program.

tags.name: Turtle Point
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-14: Also called 'Turtle Heaven' or 'Turtle City'. Location dispute: OSM places near Gili Trawangan but most sources identify Turtle Heaven/Turtle City at north Gili Meno. The coordinate (lat -8.3427, lon 116.0438) places this on/near east coast of Gili Trawangan, but multiple sources (trawangandive.com, gilidivers.com, terumbudivers.com) describe Turtle Heaven/Turtle Point at north/northeast Gili Meno. Coordinates may need verification against GPS. Pinnacle from 10m to 35m. Green and hawksbill turtles in large numbers confirmed by multiple sources. Cannot fully validate due to coordinate/location dispute.
